perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year unless terminated earlier in accordance with the terms of this Agreement (the "Term").
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be perpetually renewed every year unless terminated earlier in accordance with the terms of this Agreement (the "Term").
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states the term is for one year. Changing the definition to 'perpetually renewed' contradicts the section on Early Termination (Section 21), which implies a finite term that can be ended. This makes the actual duration of the agreement unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties may terminate its performance of related obligations under this Agreement within thirty (30) days of receipt by the Party of written termination notice.
perturbation.justification: The change from a fixed one-year term to a 'perpetually renewed' term directly contradicts the clause allowing termination with 30 days' notice. The 'perpetually renewed' definition implies an indefinite term, while the termination clause implies a finite term that can be ended, creating a significant inconsistency regarding the agreement's duration and enforceability. This falls under the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition as the perpetually renewed term and early termination cause conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: All notices and other communications required or permitted hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been duly given (a) when delivered in person, (b) five (5) business days after being sent by registered or certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, (c) when dispatched by electronic facsimile transmission (with confirmation of successful transmission), or (d) one (1) business day after having been dispatched by an internationally recognized overnight courier service, in each case to the appropriate Party at the address or facsimile number specified below:
perturbation.changed_text: All notices and other communications required or permitted hereunder shall be deemed to have been duly given (a) when delivered in person, (b) five (5) business days after being sent by registered or certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, (c) when dispatched by electronic facsimile transmission (regardless of confirmation of successful transmission), or (d) one (1) business day after having been dispatched by an internationally recognized overnight courier service, in each case to the appropriate Party at the address or facsimile number specified below:
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for 'confirmation of successful transmission' for electronic facsimile notices, this contradicts the initial statement about 'duly given'. A notice could be considered duly given even if it wasn't received by the other party, creating uncertainty. This weakens the enforceability of other sections that rely on proper notice, specifically Early Termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
All notices and other communications required or permitted hereunder shall be deemed to have been duly given (a) when delivered in person, (b) five (5) business days after being sent by registered or certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, (c) when dispatched by electronic facsimile transmission (regardless of confirmation of successful transmission), or (d) one (1) business day after having been dispatched by an internationally recognized overnight courier service, in each case to the appropriate Party at the address or facsimile number specified below:

If to HPIL ET: HPIL ENERGYTECH Inc.
Attn.: Louis Bertoli, President and CEO
7075 Gratiot Road, Suite One
Saginaw, Michigan 48609
United States of America
Facsimile No.: 001(248)750-1016
with a copy (which shall not constitute notice) to the following e-mail addresses: info@hpilenergytech.com
If to the GINARES: GINARES GROUP AG
Attn.: Peter Zu Sayn-Wittgenstein, President and CEO
Churerstrasse 47
Pfaeffikon 8808
Switzerland
Facsimile No.: +41(55)511-0810
with a copy (which shall not constitute notice) to the following e-mail address: info@ginares.com
Any Party hereto may change its address or facsimile number for the purposes of this Section 13 by giving notice as provided herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text required confirmation of successful transmission for electronic facsimile notices to be considered 'duly given'. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text repeats the changed text. This creates a contradiction because a notice could be considered 'duly given' even if it was never received, which introduces uncertainty and potential disputes regarding proper notice, impacting enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the Parties shall pay their own expenses associated with this Agreement and the transactions contemplated herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the Parties shall pay their own expenses associated with this Agreement and the transactions contemplated herein, except for expenses related to arbitration which shall be split equally between the parties regardless of the outcome.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states each party pays their own expenses. By adding an exception for arbitration expenses being split equally, this directly contradicts the original statement. This creates uncertainty regarding which expenses each party is responsible for, specifically creating a potential conflict with Section 16 which discusses arbitration and potential cost reimbursement to the prevailing party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any and all disputes or controversies between the Parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be finally settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce; provided, a Party may seek a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction, or other provisional judicial relief if in its judgment such action is necessary to avoid irreparable damage or to preserve the status quo. Despite any such action for provisional relief, the Parties will continue to participate in good faith in the procedures specified in this Section 16. Each Party shall appoint one arbitrator who shall mutually appoint a third arbitrator who shall be the sole arbitrator for the proceeding. The arbitration shall be held, and any award shall be rendered, in Paris (France), in the English language. The award may include reimbursement of the costs of the arbitration (including, without limitation, reasonable attorney fees) to the prevailing Party or a portion of such costs as determined by the arbitrator. An award of the arbitrator shall be final and binding on the Parties and judgment upon the award rendered by the arbitrator may be entered by any court having jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that each party bears its own expenses. The changed text introduces an exception where arbitration expenses are split equally. This directly contradicts the earlier statement and creates ambiguity, especially when read in conjunction with Section 16, which allows for reimbursement of arbitration costs to the prevailing party. The original clause created a reasonable expectation that regardless of arbitration, a party would bear it's own expenses. The Change modifies this by creating an exception, triggering a contradiction.
